hypothesis: If a computational framework combining fragment-based polymer generation, DFT-refined force field parameters, multi-stage MD/GCMC simulations, and Bayesian optimization with pre-trained molecular representations is applied to the chemical space of polyamines, then it will efficiently identify high-performance polyamine structures with superior solid-state CO2 capture capacity within a limited simulation budget, because the surrogate-guided search exploits transferable molecular embeddings to prioritize promising candidates without exhaustively evaluating the full combinatorial space.
